title: Excitatory and inhibitory subnetworks are equally selective during decision-making and emerge simultaneously during learning
abstract: Using two-photon calcium imaging in mouse cortex during perceptual decision-making, this study shows that excitatory and inhibitory subnetworks are equally selective and emerge simultaneously during learning, challenging models where inhibition merely balances excitation.
notes: Author list and year need verification; paper may have been published in 2020 rather than 2018 preprint. Tolias lab work on functional subnetwork selectivity.
